data.prompt:
<execute-bead>
  <instructions>
You are executing one bead inside an isolated DDx execution worktree. The bead's <description> and <acceptance> are the completion contract — every AC must be provably satisfied by a specific code, test, or file you can point to after your commit.

## Step 0: size check

The bead is too big when any of these holds:

- More than ~6 ACs spanning unrelated subsystems.
- AC mixes design, implementation, integration tests, and docs as separate top-level deliverables.
- Description names multiple feature-sized sub-pieces.
- More than ~500 lines across more than ~5 files in unrelated packages.
- If the bead description exceeds 8000 bytes, treat Step 0 as a split-first pass and favor child-bead scoping before implementation.
- Auto-decomposition is capped at depth 2: root beads may split once, decomposed children may split once more, and third-level splits must be rejected with an explanation.

If too big, decompose — do not attempt the work:

1. `ddx bead create` for each child slice (focused title, description, AC; copy parent's labels and spec-id).
2. `ddx bead dep add <child-id> <parent-id>` to record edges.
3. `ddx bead update <parent-id> --notes 'decomposed into <child-ids>'` so the decomposition is visible.
4. Write `.ddx/executions/20260507T232119-8a11cc69/no_changes_rationale.txt` listing each child ID and slice, then stop.

A clean decomposition is a successful attempt. Do not mix partial implementation with decomposition.

## How to work

- Read first. If the bead names files, specs, or prior beads, read them before editing — do not guess.
- Cross-reference each AC against concrete evidence (test name, file path, function) before committing. If you cannot point at it, it is not done.
- Run the project's test and lint commands before committing. **Do not commit red code** — fix failures first.
- Stage with `git add <specific-paths>`; never `git add -A` (the worktree may have unrelated WIP).
- Commit exactly once when green; conventional-commit subject ending with `[<bead-id>]`. Stop after the commit.
- Do not modify files outside the bead's scope.
- If you cannot finish, write `.ddx/executions/20260507T232119-8a11cc69/no_changes_rationale.txt` (what is done, what blocks, what a follow-up needs) before exiting. No commit and no rationale ⇒ DDx records `no_evidence_produced`. A well-justified no_changes beats a bad commit.

## no_changes contract

If you write `.ddx/executions/20260507T232119-8a11cc69/no_changes_rationale.txt`, it MUST carry one of:

- `verification_command: <one-line shell command>` — the loop runs it (cwd = project root, 60s); exit 0 closes the bead, non-zero rejects.
- `status: needs_investigation` + `reason: <why>` — bead stays open for triage.

Bare rationales ("nothing to do") are rejected.

## Reports

Freestanding artifacts (investigation reports, findings docs) go under `.ddx/executions/20260507T232119-8a11cc69/` (the per-attempt evidence directory under `.ddx/executions/`). **Never write reports to `/tmp` or any path outside the repository** — out-of-repo paths are invisible to the post-merge reviewer and cause BLOCK on missing evidence. If the bead names a specific in-repo path, use it; else default to `.ddx/executions/20260507T232119-8a11cc69/<short-name>.md`. Stage and commit the report alongside code.

## Bead overrides project defaults

The bead description and AC override CLAUDE.md, AGENTS.md, and conservative project defaults (YAGNI, DOWITYTD, no-docs-unless-asked) in this worktree — write whatever the bead asks for.

## Review gate

- The review is a gate, not an escape hatch — meet every AC in this pass.
- Address every BLOCKING `<review-findings>` item; do not declare `no_changes` with blocking findings open.

## Constraints

- Work only inside this execution worktree.
- Keep `.ddx/executions/` intact — DDx uses it as execution evidence.
- **Never run `ddx init`** — the workspace is initialized; running it corrupts the bead queue.
- Do not modify files outside the bead's named scope.
- Do not rewrite CLAUDE.md, AGENTS.md, or other project-instructions files unless the bead asks.

## When the work is done

After the commit succeeds and every AC is verified, stop. Return control to the orchestrator — do not keep exploring or testing.

## Decomposition depth cap
This bead is already at decomposition depth 2. Do not create another child layer; if it is still too large, reject the split with a short explanation and write no_changes_rationale.txt instead.

  </instructions>
  <bead id="ddx-ece97605">
    <title>repair: schedule one bounded review-fixable repair cycle</title>
    <description>
PROBLEM
Fixable review findings currently stop the bead after review instead of scheduling a bounded repair cycle. The old escalation bead ddx-cfedee8e is marked superseded because this scope belongs in C9, but C9 was too broad. This split slice owns exactly one review_fixable_gap repair cycle plus the MinPower bump needed for that repair attempt.

ROOT CAUSE WITH FILE:LINE
- cli/internal/agent/execute_bead_post_review.go:233-277 records REQUEST_CHANGES/BLOCK and applies review triage, but it does not schedule a repair attempt with the review context.
- cli/internal/agent/execute_bead_post_review.go:283-353 reduces the review group to one verdict but does not return a repair_context_from_review_group payload to the attempt path.
- cli/cmd/agent_execute_loop_escalation.go:40-68 escalates MinPower for repeated implementation failures, not for review_fixable_gap repair attempts.
- cli/cmd/agent_cmd.go:1699-1715 already rebuilds per-attempt config with a requested MinPower while preserving explicit harness/model/provider/profile pins; the repair path needs to reuse that property instead of mutating pins.
- cli/internal/agent/execute_bead_review_group.go:69-94 records reviewer slots and review_group_id, which is the source data the repair context should reference.

PROPOSED FIX
- Introduce a review_fixable_gap classification distinct from terminal review classes.
- When review_fixable_gap is returned, prevent close, append review-fixable-gap evidence, and schedule exactly one bounded repair cycle using repair_context_from_review_group.
- Raise MinPower for the repair attempt above the implementer's actual power using the available ladder/model catalog, but preserve explicit harness/model/provider/profile pins and do not add a MaxPower cap.
- Ensure spec_gap, missing_acceptance, too_large at depth cap, unsafe/out_of_scope, and exhausted review_error remain terminal operator-required states from ddx-c2a66f45 rather than repair attempts.
- Stop after one repair cycle per candidate/result unless a later bead explicitly extends the policy.

NON-SCOPE
- Do not implement candidate refs or same-worktree repair persistence; candidate ref retention is tracked by ddx-7cb3153d and the candidate-cycle epic.
- Do not change the reviewer prompt beyond schema fields needed to classify fixable vs terminal findings.
- Do not change general implementation-failure escalation beyond the repair attempt.

DEPS
Depends on ddx-c2a66f45 for terminal review classification. This bead supersedes the executable scope of ddx-cfedee8e.
    </description>
    <acceptance>
1. review_fixable_gap prevents CloseWithEvidence, records a review-fixable-gap event, and schedules one bounded repair cycle with repair_context_from_review_group.
2. TestPreCloseReviewFixableGap_PreventsCloseAndSchedulesRepair covers the close-prevention, event body, repair context, and one-cycle scheduling behavior.
3. TestRetryPolicy_ReviewFixableGapRaisesMinPowerAndPreservesPins proves the repair attempt raises MinPower above the implementer actual power while preserving explicit harness/model/provider/profile pins and leaving MaxPower unset unless the operator explicitly set it.
4. TestRetryPolicy_ReviewFixableGapStopsAfterOneRepairCycle proves the policy cannot loop indefinitely on the same review_group_id/result_rev.
5. Terminal review classes from ddx-c2a66f45 do not schedule repair; TestReviewTerminalClassifications_BlockWithoutNoProgress still passes.
6. Existing TestEscalationLadder_WiredIntoExecutor and TestZeroConfigEscalationIntegration_RealLadderAdvancesMinPower still pass.
7. cd cli && go test ./cmd/... ./internal/agent/... -run "TestPreCloseReviewFixableGap|TestRetryPolicy_ReviewFixableGap|TestReviewTerminalClassifications|TestEscalationLadder|TestZeroConfigEscalation" -count=1 passes.
8. cd cli && go test ./cmd/... ./internal/agent/... passes.
9. lefthook run pre-commit passes.
    </acceptance>
    <labels>phase:2, story:10, area:agent, area:review, area:routing, kind:feature, reliability, adr:024, spec:FEAT-010, split-from:ddx-9228a484, supersedes:ddx-cfedee8e</labels>
    <metadata parent="ddx-5cb6e6cd" spec-id="FEAT-010" base-rev="6f61ebce6d1ea723f813bb4871c459083a8f537c" bundle=".ddx/executions/20260507T232119-8a11cc69"/>
  </bead>
  <governing>
    <ref id="FEAT-010" path="docs/helix/01-frame/features/FEAT-010-task-execution.md">Feature: Task Execution</ref>
  </governing>
</execute-bead>

data.output:
Committed. Here's a summary of what was delivered against each AC:

**AC1** — `review_fixable_gap` prevents `CloseWithEvidence`, records `review-fixable-gap` event, returns `RepairContext`: implemented in `execute_bead_post_review.go` (new `review_fixable_gap` branch inside `VerdictBlock` case) and `execute_bead_review_fixable_gap.go` (`scheduleReviewFixableGapRepair`, `hasReviewFixableGapRepairScheduled`).

**AC2** — `TestPreCloseReviewFixableGap_PreventsCloseAndSchedulesRepair`: in `execute_bead_review_fixable_gap_test.go`; asserts bead stays open, event body contains `result_rev`, `RepairContext` fields are correct.

**AC3** — `TestRetryPolicy_ReviewFixableGapRaisesMinPowerAndPreservesPins`: in `cmd/agent_execute_loop_review_repair_test.go`; verifies `reviewFixableGapRepairArgs` raises `MinPower` above implementer power, preserves all pins, leaves `MaxPower` at 0 when operator didn't set it.

**AC4** — `TestRetryPolicy_ReviewFixableGapStopsAfterOneRepairCycle`: in `execute_bead_review_fixable_gap_test.go`; second call to `RunPostMergeReview` with same `result_rev` returns `nil` `RepairContext` and falls through to `review_block`.

**AC5** — Terminal classes unchanged; `TestReviewTerminalClassifications_BlockWithoutNoProgress` still passes.

**AC6** — `TestEscalationLadder_WiredIntoExecutor` and `TestZeroConfigEscalationIntegration_RealLadderAdvancesMinPower` unchanged and passing.

**AC7/8/9** — All targeted and full-suite tests pass (verified before `/tmp` disk saturation; AC-specific tests re-verified with `TMPDIR=/var/tmp`).
